youtube.name: YouTube
privacy_requirements: Must not upload content that violates individual's privacy
Faces of private individuals should be blurred if identifiable
License plates should be blurred for privacy protection
Content scraped from YouTube must comply with YouTube TOS Section 5B
No unauthorized use of personal data from videos

tiktok.name: TikTok
privacy_requirements: Personal data of users must not be collected without consent
Faces and identifying information must be anonymized for dataset use

cvpr2026.name: CVPR 2026
dataset_requirements: Datasets must comply with all applicable privacy laws (GDPR, CCPA, etc.)
Identifiable individuals in datasets must be anonymized or have given explicit consent
Authors must confirm compliance with data collection platform terms of service
Privacy-sensitive regions (faces, license plates) must be de-identified
Dataset paper must include IRB approval or equivalent ethics review
Must provide documentation of anonymization methodology
cvpr2026.notes: Ego-centric / first-person videos require stricter processing with egoblur tool

cvpr2025.name: CVPR 2025
dataset_requirements: GDPR compliance mandatory for EU-sourced data

iccv2025.name: ICCV 2025
dataset_requirements: Privacy compliance required for all human subjects
Data collection must comply with platform terms of service

neurips2025.name: NeurIPS 2025
dataset_requirements: Dataset and Benchmark papers require ethics statement
Human subjects data must be anonymized

gdpr.name: GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation)
requirements: Personal data must not be processed without legal basis
Biometric data (faces) is 'special category' requiring explicit consent or legal basis
Right to be forgotten: individuals can request removal of their data
Data minimization: only collect data necessary for the purpose
Anonymization must be irreversible to be outside GDPR scope
gdpr.notes: GDPR requires irreversible anonymization. Generative face replacement preferred over simple blur for compliance.

ccpa.name: CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act)
requirements: Consumers have right to know what personal information is collected
Consumers can opt-out of sale of personal information

conflict_resolution.notes: When conflicts exist, stricter requirement prevails. GDPR irreversibility > simple blur.
